candidates.why: Represents core operating revenue; values match Total Revenue, suggesting both are alternative labels for the same top-line figure.
Explicit total revenue line; identical values to Operating revenue, making it a clear candidate for top-line sales.

variables.notes: Both candidate rows are valid revenue; using Total Revenue as primary and Operating revenue as fallback per priority rule.
No specific cost of goods sold or cost of sales line is provided among candidates.

candidates.why: Broad operating expense bucket likely including SG&A-type overhead in absence of a dedicated SG&A line.
Explicit overhead-type operating expenses, suitable as SG&A component.
Clearly an SG&A-type administrative overhead expense.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A line; using main overhead buckets per hierarchy rule, excluding any totals or D&A/impairment lines.
No R&D-related line is present in the candidate list; treating XRD as missing (0).

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line on income statement with negative values, best representation of financing cost.
Represents allocation of profit to minority holders, not an interest expense; should not be used for XINT.
Represents profit attributable to parent shareholders, not an interest expense; not suitable for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental interest expense on lease liabilities; suitable as secondary interest-expense source if main line missing for some years.
Balance sheet equity for minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Balance sheet equity for minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Represents accrued (unpaid) interest on balance sheet; can serve as a tertiary proxy if income statement interest labels change, though not ideal.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Non-controlling/Minority interest items are equity allocations, not interest. Priority list follows main interest expense, then lease interest supplement, then accrued interest as last resort.

candidates.why: Component of shareholders’ equity but not the total; prefer a total equity line.
Appears to be another component bucket of equity; not the overall total.
Explicit total shareholders’ equity attributable to owners of the parent, which corresponds to book equity excluding minorities.
Total equity including non-controlling interests; useful as backup if shareholders’ equity label changes.
Balancing adjustment row, not an economic equity measure.
Explicit balancing item, not to be used as BE when proper totals exist.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing line for the accounting identity; not book equity itself.
Represents total assets/total liabilities and equity, not equity alone.

variables.notes: Using Shareholders' Equity - Total as primary book equity (attributable to owners), with Total Equity as fallback in case of label changes.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to non-controlling interests; reflects flow, not stock, but confirms presence of minority interests.
Balance sheet carrying amount of equity attributable to non-controlling interests; best representation of minority interest (MIB).
Alternate label for the same non-controlling equity; useful as backup if primary label unavailable in some years.
Income statement minority interest in profit; again a flow item, not the stock measure for MIB.

variables.notes: MIB is defined as balance sheet minority equity; using Non-Controlling Interests as primary and Minority Interest as fallback.
